query: Microsoft 365 Copilot declarative agents updated GPT model version automatic model selection

mako.raw:
{"query":"Microsoft 365 Copilot declarative agents updated GPT model version automatic model selection","model":"mako","response_time_ms":2602,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-365/copilot/release-notes","title":"Release Notes for Microsoft 365 Copilot | Microsoft Learn","content":"Open the Copilot Prompt Gallery in the Microsoft 365 admin center.
Review your Confluence or ServiceNow catalog permissions.
Connect 15Five with Microsoft 365 Copilot to bring team priorities and recognition into Copilot experiences.
PowerPoint now supports Black Forest Lab's FLUX.2 Flex image generation model, giving users a new option for creating high quality visuals.
What changed: Users can now select FLUX.2 Flex when generating images in PowerPoint.
Organizational Messages in the Microsoft 365 Admin Center now supports email delivery in addition to other surfaces like the Windows Taskbar, Windows Spotlight, Windows Notification Center, and Teams Popovers.
GPT 5.5 Thinking expands what Copilot Chat can do by enabling deeper reasoning and more complex problem solving.
What changed: Microsoft 365 Copilot now supports an Asana Copilot connector that can index Asana tasks for use in Microsoft 365 Copilot and Microsoft Search.
Business impact: Improves project tracking by bringing Asana task data into Microsoft 365 Copilot and search experiences.
Microsoft 365 Copilot declarative agents now use the GPT-5.1 model with automatic model selection for performance and reasoning.
Select the refresh option on an Adaptive Card inside your agent.
Use the updated card to continue your workflow with the latest information.
What Changed: You can now connect an existing Foundry agent to Microsoft 365 Copilot and Microsoft Teams via a proxy app built with the Microsoft 365 Agents Toolkit.
Integrate your Microsoft Foundry agent with Microsoft 365 using Agents Toolkit sample.","date":"3 days ago"},{"url":"https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/microsoft-copilot-studio/authoring-select-agent-model","title":"Select a primary AI model for your agent - Microsoft Copilot Studio | Microsoft Learn","content":"Separate settings exist for changing models for deep reasoning (preview), generative responses (preview), and the prompt builder.
Copilot Studio offers different types of models.
These model types are based on their intended use and availability.
Generally available (GA): Models without a release tag are generally available.
Feel free to experiment with these models.
Learn more in the Microsoft 365 admin center documentation:"},{"url":"https://team400.ai/blog/2026-06-copilot-declarative-agents-gpt-5-1-model-changes","title":"What the GPT 5.1+ Model Change Means for Your Microsoft 365 Copilot Agents | Team 400 Blog","content":"Then Microsoft moves the underlying model to a newer version, and one morning your carefully behaved agent starts doing something subtly different.
Run them against the agent on the new model and compare the answers to what you expected.
If you're running declarative agents on Microsoft 365 Copilot and this migration has you a bit nervous about what might shift, that's a healthy instinct."}]}

tavily.raw: {"query":"Microsoft 365 Copilot declarative agents updated GPT model version automatic model selection","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/blog/microsoft365copilotblog/microsoft-365-copilot-declarative-agents-are-getting-smarter-with-gpt%E2%80%915-2/4504774","title":"Microsoft 365 Copilot Declarative Agents Are Getting ...","content":"Mar 24, 2026 — Copilot Declarative Agents are upgrading to GPT‐5.2, bringing improved reasoning, more reliable tool use, Declarative Agents are being updated","score":0.8259413,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://techcommunity.microsoft.com/discussions/microsoft365copilot/has-model-selection-been-removed-from-microsoft-365-copilot/4534914","title":"Has model selection been removed from Microsoft 365 Copilot? | Microsoft Community Hub","content":"## Forum Discussion. # Has model selection been removed from Microsoft 365 Copilot? I noticed that the option to manually select GPT models in Microsoft 365 Copilot seems to be gone, or at least no longer visible. I have also seen similar concerns discussed on Reddit. Could Microsoft clarify the current status? - Has manual model selection been removed? - How can users know which model Copilot is currently using? - Is Copilot still using stronger models such as GPT-5.5, or only automatic routing now? - Why does “Deep Analysis” / “Think Deeper” appear to switch back to Auto mode? For corporate use, this lack of transparency is concerning. If I cannot see or control which model is being used, it reduces trust in the quality of the output and makes me more cautious about what tasks I rely on Copilot for. My understanding is that you're not going to get those by ...Read more","score":0.66621506,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://community.powerplatform.com/forums/thread/details?threadid=4755681a-4749-f111-bec7-7ced8dcd240d","title":"Power Platform Community Forum Thread Details","content":"# M365 Copilot App Model Selector Active Inside Published Copilot Studio Agent — By Design or UI Gap? When you publish a Copilot Studio agent to the M365 Copilot app, the agent’s orchestration and AI model selection inside Copilot Studio (e.g., Claude Sonnet 4.6) is fixed for that agent’s backend processing. However, the M365 Copilot app itself includes a **platform-level model selector** that remains active and allows users to override or choose different models (Auto / Quick Response / Think Deeper / specific models) at runtime. This model selector in the M365 Copilot app is a user-facing control that applies on top of the agent’s configured model, effectively allowing users to influence the model behavior dynamically. The Copilot Studio agent’s configured model acts as the default or baseline, but the M365 Copilot app’s selector can override it for the session or query. This design provides flexibility for end users to adjust model depth or style without republishing the agent, but it can cause confusion if you expect the agent’s model choice to be locked.","score":0.5331307,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.67,"request_id":"326a6832-442f-4a70-ab08-68338a0bdf72"}
